---
title: "Multicoil Phase Features"
---

A single periodic feature is one circle. A tuple of periodic features is a
finite product of circles, and the joint repeat horizon is the least common
multiple of their periods.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Goal

Learn how Circle Calculus packages multi-period phase tags as explicit
finite features with theorem-backed repeat and relative-shift checks.

:::

## Concept

For periods `(p1, p2, ..., pk)`, a position becomes a phase tuple:

```text
position -> (position mod p1, position mod p2, ..., position mod pk)
```

The tuple repeats after the joint horizon. For two positions, a relative phase
can stay invariant under a common shift even when absolute positions change.

## Example

The public fixture uses periods `(5, 7)` and position `37`:

```text
phase tuple = (2, 2)
joint repeat horizon = 35
```

Shifting by `35` returns the same tuple. The relative phase between a query and
key is also preserved when both are shifted by the same amount.

```bash
python scripts/multicoil_phase_feature_certify.py --format json
python scripts/circle_ai_contract_ready.py --kind multicoil_phase_feature --digest --field joint_repeat_horizon --field relative_phase --include-recommendations
```

## Theorem Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIA-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIA-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIA-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIT-T0004"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIT-T0005"></div>

## Certificate Reading

The useful fields are:

- `periods`: the declared finite phase bank.
- `phase_tuple`: the phase tag for the selected position.
- `joint_repeat_horizon`: the repeat horizon for the full tuple.
- `relative_phase`: the query-key phase difference in the declared period.
- `shifted_relative_phase`: the same value after a common shift.

Planner recommendations:

- `PHASE-USE-JOINT-REPEAT-HORIZON` says a downstream system can use the joint horizon as an explicit repeat boundary.
- `PHASE-AUDIT-RELATIVE-SHIFT-INVARIANT` says query-key features should preserve relative phase under common shifts when that invariance is expected.

::: {.warning-box}
This contract proves finite phase-feature bookkeeping and invariance facts. It
does not prove that these features improve a model, choose the right periods,
or discover periodic structure in real data. Python fixtures are executable
references, not proof artifacts and not performance claims.
:::

## Dictionary

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0076"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0077"></div>

## Source Trail

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_01_CIRCLE_AI_ARCHITECTURES.md">Architecture paper: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_01_CIRCLE_AI_ARCHITECTURES.md">Circle AI Architectures</a></p>

<p>Quickstart: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/docs/MULTICOIL_PHASE_FEATURE_CERTIFIER_QUICKSTART.md">Multicoil Phase Feature Certifier</a></p>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="cyclic_memory_residue_winding.qmd">Previous: Cyclic Memory Residue Winding</a>
<a href="circulant_block_cyclic_mixer.qmd">Next: Circulant And Block-Cyclic Mixers</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
